1. <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
2.   <PropertyGroup>
3.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
4.   </PropertyGroup>
5. 
6.   <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
7.     <PropertyGroup>
8.       <TimestampFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt</TimestampFile>
9.     </PropertyGroup>
10.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
11.   </Target>
12. 
13.   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
14.     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
15.       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
16.     </Exec>
17.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
18.                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
19.                      Overwrite="true" />
20.     <ItemGroup>
21.       <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
22.     </ItemGroup>
23.   </Target>
24. </Project>
25.

     </PropertyGroup>
     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
   </Target>
 
   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
     </Exec>
     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
                      Overwrite="true" />
     <ItemGroup>
       <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
     </ItemGroup>
   </Target>
 </Project>

data.content:
## Incremental Build Issues Found

This project has **two custom targets** that break incremental builds:

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6–11)

**Problem:** Writes a file with `DateTime.Now` on every build. Since the timestamp always changes, the output file is always modified, which invalidates downstream targets (like `CoreCompile`) even when source code hasn't changed.

**Fix:** Either remove it, or add `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes so MSBuild can skip it when inputs haven't changed. A timestamp that changes every build is fundamentally incompatible with incrementality — consider making it opt-in (e.g., only in CI).

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13–23)

**Problems:**
- **No `Inputs`/`Outputs`** — MSBuild will always re-run this target since it can't determine if it's up-to-date.
- **Unconditionally rewrites `git-hash.g.cs`** — even if the git hash hasn't changed, it overwrites the file (updating its timestamp), which forces recompilation.
- **Adds `<Compile>` items at execution time** — this can confuse IDEs and the Fast Up-to-Date Check in Visual Studio.

**Fix:** Add `Inputs`/`Outputs` and use `WriteLinesToFile` conditionally (only write when content differs), or use a marker/cache file to detect when the hash actually changes.

---

**Summary:** Both targets run unconditionally on every build and produce outputs that force `CoreCompile` to re-execute, meaning you never get a true no-op build even when nothing has changed.
